root_text.sn47.26:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno āyasmā ca anuruddho sākete viharanti kaṇḍakīvane.
root_text.sn47.26:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhitā yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā anuruddhena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn47.26:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.26:1.5: Kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, sekho hotī”ti?
root_text.sn47.26:1.6: “Catunnaṁ kho, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ padesaṁ bhāvitattā sekho hoti.
root_text.sn47.26:2.2: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.26:2.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.26:2.6: Imesaṁ kho, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ padesaṁ bhāvitattā sekho hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn47.26:1.1: At one time the venerables Sāriputta, Mahāmoggallāna, and Anuruddha were staying near Sāketa, in the Thorny Wood.
translation_text.sn47.26:1.2: Then in the late afternoon, Sāriputta and Mahāmoggallāna came out of retreat, went to Anuruddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn47.26:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side. Sāriputta said to Anuruddha:
translation_text.sn47.26:1.4: “Reverend, they speak of the one called ‘a trainee’.
translation_text.sn47.26:1.6: “Reverends, a trainee is someone who has partly developed the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.26:2.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.26:2.3: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.26:2.5: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.26:2.6: A trainee is someone who has partly developed the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.”
